title: carex sartwellii
intro: Carex sartwellii, also known as Sartwell's sedge, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Cyperaceae, which is native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to its saponins and flavonoids content. The health benefits of Carex sartwellii include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of Carex sartwellii can be made by drying and infusing the leaves and roots, and can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal purposes.
